title: cerastium arvense subsp. lineare
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. lineare, also known as the Sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of the annual or biennial herb Cerastium arvense. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense subsp. lineare include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams, and is also used in homeopathic remedies.
